name: Instagram Post Reel from URL
description: Verifies the correct Instagram account is active, downloads a video from a URL into a unique album, then uploads it as a Reel with a caption.

params.username: The Instagram username that should be active
params.videoUrl: Direct URL to the video file to download
params.caption: Caption text for the Reel
params.albumName: Short unique album name (e.g. 'r3a8f1' — 6-8 chars to avoid ellipsis truncation in Instagram UI)

steps.goal: Check which Instagram account is currently logged in by looking at the profile tab or the username displayed at the top. If the active account is '{{username}}', proceed (mark done). If a different account is active, tap the username at the top to open the account switcher, then select '{{username}}'. If '{{username}}' is not listed, mark as failed.

Download a video file to the device into a specific album. Use the download action with url {{videoUrl}} and save it as {{albumName}}/reel_video.mp4. The album format (AlbumName/filename) will create a gallery album named '{{albumName}}' under Pictures. After the download completes, mark done.

Open Instagram. Tap on the Home icon on the bottom left. Tap the '+' icon at the top left of the screen to open the new post/reel creation screen. If you see a media gallery with tabs at the bottom (POST, REEL, STORY), tap the 'REEL' tab to switch to Reel mode.

You are on the Reel creation screen and can see a media gallery. At the very top of the gallery area there is a small dropdown button — it likely says 'Recents' with a small downward arrow or chevron. Tap that 'Recents' dropdown button. Do NOT scroll through the media grid or the feed. Only tap the dropdown label at the top of the gallery.

A dropdown or bottom sheet with album options is now visible. Find and tap the option labeled 'All albums' (or 'Other...' or 'Browse folders'). This should reveal the complete list of all photo/video albums on the device. Scroll through the albums and find the one named '{{albumName}}', then tap it to select it.

You should now see the media from the '{{albumName}}' album. There should be one video — select it by tapping on it. After selecting, you may see a trim/edit screen. Tap 'Next' or the forward arrow to proceed to the next screen.

You may see an 'About Reels' or informational popup/dialog. If so, dismiss it by tapping outside the popup, tapping the 'X' close button, or tapping any dismiss/OK button. If there is no popup, proceed. You should now be on the caption/share screen. Look for the text field that says 'Write a caption...' or 'Write a caption and add hashtags...'. Tap on that text field to focus it, then type the following caption exactly: '{{caption}}'. Make sure the caption text appears in the field before marking done.

The caption should now be filled in. Tap the 'Share' button (usually blue, at the bottom of the screen) to publish the Reel. Wait for the upload to begin — you may see a progress indicator or the app may navigate back to the feed. Once the upload has started, mark done.
